title: Immigration laws are the only laws
body:
## "Immigration laws are the only laws that are discussed in terms of how to help people who break them."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Immigration laws are the only laws that are discussed in terms of how to help people who break them."

title: 'Income distribution'
body:
## "Despite a voluminous and often fervent literature on 'income distribution', the cold fact is that most income is not distributed: It is earned."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Despite a voluminous and often fervent literature on 'income distribution', the cold fact is that most income is not distributed: It is earned."

title: It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions
body:
## "It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions than by putting those decisions in the hands of people who pay no price for being wrong."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions than by putting those decisions in the hands of people who pay no price for being wrong."

title: It is usually futile to try to talk
body:
## "It is usually futile to try to talk facts and analysis to people who are enjoying a sense of moral superiority in their ignorance."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "It is usually futile to try to talk facts and analysis to people who are enjoying a sense of moral superiority in their ignorance."

title: It was Thomas Edison who brought
body:
## "It was Thomas Edison who brought us electricity not the Sierra Club. It was the Wright Brothers who got us off the ground not the Federal Aviation Administration.

## It was Henry Ford who ended the isolation of millions of Americans by making the automobile affordable, not Ralph Nader.

## Those who have helped the poor the most have not been those who have gone around loudly expressing compassion for the poor but those who found ways to make industry more productive and distribution more efficient so that the poor of today can afford things that the affluent of yesterday could only dream about."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "It was Thomas Edison who brought us electricity not the Sierra Club. It was the Wright Brothers who got us off the ground not the Federal Aviation Administration.

title: It's amazing how fast people learn
body:
## "It's amazing how fast people learn when they are not insulated from the consequences of their decisions."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "It's amazing how fast people learn when they are not insulated from the consequences of their decisions."

title: It is fascinating to watch politicians
body:
## "It is fascinating to watch politicians come up with 'solutions' to problems that are a direct result of their previous solutions. In many cases the most efficient thing to do would be to repeal their previous solution and stop being so gung-ho for creating new solutions."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "It is fascinating to watch politicians come up with 'solutions' to problems that are a direct result of their previous solutions. In many cases the most efficient thing to do would be to repeal their previous solution and stop being so gung-ho for creating new solutions."

title: Many on the political left
body:
## "Many on the political left are so entranced by the beauty of their vision that they cannot see the ugly reality they are creating in the real world."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Many on the political left are so entranced by the beauty of their vision that they cannot see the ugly reality they are creating in the real world."

title: Morally and intellectually superior
body:
## "The real motives of liberals have nothing to do with the welfare of other people. Instead, they have two related goals — to establish themselves as morally and intellectually superior to the rather distasteful population of common people, and to gather as much power as possible to tell those distasteful common people how they must live their lives."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The real motives of liberals have nothing to do with the welfare of other people. Instead, they have two related goals — to establish themselves as morally and intellectually superior to the rather distasteful population of common people, and to gather as much power as possible to tell those distasteful common people how they must live their lives."

title: More whites were brought as slaves
body:
## "More whites were brought as slaves to North Africa than blacks brought as slaves to the United States or to the 13 colonies from which it was formed. White slaves were still being bought and sold in the Ottoman Empire, decades after blacks were freed in the United States."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "More whites were brought as slaves to North Africa than blacks brought as slaves to the United States or to the 13 colonies from which it was formed. White slaves were still being bought and sold in the Ottoman Empire, decades after blacks were freed in the United States."

title: Much of what are called 'social problems'
body:
## "Much of what are called 'social problems' consists of the fact that intellectuals have theories that do not fit the real world. From this they conclude that it is the real world which is wrong and needs changing."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Much of what are called 'social problems' consists of the fact that intellectuals have theories that do not fit the real world. From this they conclude that it is the real world which is wrong and needs changing."

title: Multiculturalism, like the caste system
body:
## "Multiculturalism, like the caste system, tends to freeze people where the accident of birth has placed them."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Multiculturalism, like the caste system, tends to freeze people where the accident of birth has placed them."

title: No matter how disastrously some policy
body:
## "No matter how disastrously some policy has turned out, anyone who criticizes it can expect to hear: 'But what would you replace it with?' When you put out a fire, what do you replace it with?"

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "No matter how disastrously some policy has turned out, anyone who criticizes it can expect to hear: 'But what would you replace it with?' When you put out a fire, what do you replace it with?"

title: Nobody is equal to anybody
body:
## "Nobody is equal to anybody. Even the same man is not equal to himself on different days."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Nobody is equal to anybody. Even the same man is not equal to himself on different days."

title: One of many disservices done to young people
body:
## "One of many disservices done to young people is giving them the puffed up notion that they are in a position to pass sweeping judgments on a world that they have barely begun to experience."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "One of many disservices done to young people is giving them the puffed up notion that they are in a position to pass sweeping judgments on a world that they have barely begun to experience."

title: One of the most important reasons
body:
## "One of the most important reasons for studying history is that virtually every stupid idea that is in vogue today has been tried before and proved disastrous before, time and again."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "One of the most important reasons for studying history is that virtually every stupid idea that is in vogue today has been tried before and proved disastrous before, time and again."

title: Ours may become the first civilization destroyed
body:
## "Ours may become the first civilization destroyed, not by the power of our enemies, but by the ignorance of our teachers and the dangerous nonsense they are teaching our children."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Ours may become the first civilization destroyed, not by the power of our enemies, but by the ignorance of our teachers and the dangerous nonsense they are teaching our children."

title: People who are very aware
body:
## "People who are very aware that they have more knowledge than the average person are often very unaware that they do not have one-tenth of the knowledge of all of the average persons put together. In this situation, for the intelligentsia to impose their notions on ordinary people is essentially to impose ignorance on knowledge."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "People who are very aware that they have more knowledge than the average person are often very unaware that they do not have one-tenth of the knowledge of all of the average persons put together. In this situation, for the intelligentsia to impose their notions on ordinary people is essentially to impose ignorance on knowledge."

title: People who pride themselves
body:
## "People who pride themselves on their 'complexity' and deride others for being 'simplistic' should realize that the truth is often not very complicated. What gets complex is evading the truth."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "People who pride themselves on their 'complexity' and deride others for being 'simplistic' should realize that the truth is often not very complicated. What gets complex is evading the truth."

title: Racism is not dead
body:
## "Racism is not dead, but it is on life support — kept alive by politicians, race hustlers and people who get a sense of superiority by denouncing others as 'racists'."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Racism is not dead, but it is on life support — kept alive by politicians, race hustlers and people who get a sense of superiority by denouncing others as 'racists'."

title: Since wealth is the only thing
body:
## "Since wealth is the only thing that can cure poverty, you might think that the left would be as obsessed with the creation of wealth as they are with the redistribution of wealth.But you would be wrong."

Thomas Sowell

**_That's because they don't care about eradicating poverty they want power._**
quoteText: "Since wealth is the only thing that can cure poverty, you might think that the left would be as obsessed with the creation of wealth as they are with the redistribution of wealth.But you would be wrong."

title: Socialism in general has a record of failure
body:
## "Socialism in general has a record of failure so blatant that only an intellectual could ignore or evade"

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Socialism in general has a record of failure so blatant that only an intellectual could ignore or evade"

title: Some Americans will never appreciate America
body:
## "Some Americans will never appreciate America, until after they have helped destroy it, and have then begun to suffer the consequences."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Some Americans will never appreciate America, until after they have helped destroy it, and have then begun to suffer the consequences."

title: The next time some academics tell you
body:
## "The next time some academics tell you how important diversity is, ask how many Republicans there are in their sociology department."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The next time some academics tell you how important diversity is, ask how many Republicans there are in their sociology department."

title: The beauty of doing nothing
body:
## "The beauty of doing nothing is that you can do it perfectly. Only when you do something is it almost impossible to do it without mistakes. Therefore people who are contributing nothing to society, except their constant criticisms, can feel both intellectually and morally superior."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The beauty of doing nothing is that you can do it perfectly. Only when you do something is it almost impossible to do it without mistakes. Therefore people who are contributing nothing to society, except their constant criticisms, can feel both intellectually and morally superior."

title: The black family which survived slavery
body:
## "The black family survived centuries of slavery and generations of segregation, but it has disintegrated in the wake of the Liberals' expansion of the welfare state."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The black family survived centuries of slavery and generations of segregation, but it has disintegrated in the wake of the Liberals' expansion of the welfare state."

title: The fact that crime and poverty
body:
## "The fact that crime and poverty are correlated is automatically taken to mean that poverty causes crime, rather than that similar attitudes or behavior patterns may contribute to both poverty and crime."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The fact that crime and poverty are correlated is automatically taken to mean that poverty causes crime, rather than that similar attitudes or behavior patterns may contribute to both poverty and crime."

title: The first lesson of economics is scarcity
body:
## "The first lesson of economics is scarcity: there is never enough of anything to fully satisfy all those who want it. The first lesson of politics is to disregard the first lesson of economics."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The first lesson of economics is scarcity: there is never enough of anything to fully satisfy all those who want it. The first lesson of politics is to disregard the first lesson of economics."

title: The left takes its vision seriously
body:
## "The left takes its vision seriously — more seriously than it takes the rights of other people. They want to be our shepherds. But that requires us to be sheep."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The left takes its vision seriously — more seriously than it takes the rights of other people. They want to be our shepherds. But that requires us to be sheep."

title: The more I study the history of
body:
## "The more I study the history of intellectuals, the more they seem like a wrecking crew, dismantling civilization bit by bit — replacing what works with what sounds good."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The more I study the history of intellectuals, the more they seem like a wrecking crew, dismantling civilization bit by bit — replacing what works with what sounds good."

title: The most basic question is
body:
## "The most basic question is not what is best, but who shall decide what is best."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The most basic question is not what is best, but who shall decide what is best."

title: The purpose of politics
body:
## "The purpose of politics is not to solve problems but to find problems to justify the expansion of government power and an increase in taxes."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The purpose of politics is not to solve problems but to find problems to justify the expansion of government power and an increase in taxes."

title: The very same people who say
body:
## "The very same people who say that government has no right to interfere with sexual activity between consenting adults believe that the government has every right to interfere with economic activity between consenting adults."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The very same people who say that government has no right to interfere with sexual activity between consenting adults believe that the government has every right to interfere with economic activity between consenting adults."

title: There are no solutions
body:
## "There are no solutions; there are only trade-offs."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "There are no solutions; there are only trade-offs."

title: There are no solutions
body:
## "There are no solutions. There are only trade-offs."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "There are no solutions. There are only trade-offs."

title: There has now been created a world
body:
## "There has now been created a world in which the success of others is a grievance, rather than an example."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "There has now been created a world in which the success of others is a grievance, rather than an example."

title: Too much of what is called education
body:
## "Too much of what is called 'education' is little more than an expensive isolation from reality"

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Too much of what is called 'education' is little more than an expensive isolation from reality"

title: We are a nation of immigrants
body:
## "'We are a nation of immigrants,' we are constantly reminded. We are also a nation of people with ten fingers and ten toes. Does that mean that anyone who has ten fingers and ten toes should be welcomed and given American citizenship?"

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "'We are a nation of immigrants,' we are constantly reminded. We are also a nation of people with ten fingers and ten toes. Does that mean that anyone who has ten fingers and ten toes should be welcomed and given American citizenship?"

title: What do you call it when someone steals money
body:
## "What do you call it when someone steals money secretly? Theft. What do you call it when someone takes money by force? Robbery. What do you call it when a politician takes someone else's money and gives it to someone likely to vote for him? Social Justice."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "What do you call it when someone steals money secretly? Theft. What do you call it when someone takes money by force? Robbery. What do you call it when a politician takes someone else's money and gives it to someone likely to vote for him? Social Justice."

title: What exactly is your fair share
body:
## "What exactly is your fair share of what someone else has worked for?"

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "What exactly is your fair share of what someone else has worked for?"

title: What 'multiculturalism' boils down to
body:
## "What 'multiculturalism' boils down to is that you can praise any culture in the world except Western culture-and you cannot blame any culture in the world except Western culture."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "What 'multiculturalism' boils down to is that you can praise any culture in the world except Western culture-and you cannot blame any culture in the world except Western culture."

title: When I was growing up
body:
## "When I was growing up, we were taught the stories of people whose inventions and scientific discoveries had expanded the lives of millions of other people. Today, students are being taught to admire those who complain, denounce and demand."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "When I was growing up, we were taught the stories of people whose inventions and scientific discoveries had expanded the lives of millions of other people. Today, students are being taught to admire those who complain, denounce and demand."

title: Where nobody is responsible for what they did
body:
## "We seem to be getting closer and closer to a situation where nobody is responsible for what they did but we are all responsible for what somebody else did."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "We seem to be getting closer and closer to a situation where nobody is responsible for what they did but we are all responsible for what somebody else did."

title: You have to have a sense of humor
body:
## "You have to have a sense of humor if you follow politics. Otherwise, the sheer fraudulence of it all will get you down."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "You have to have a sense of humor if you follow politics. Otherwise, the sheer fraudulence of it all will get you down."

title: Those who would give up essential liberty
body:
## "Those who would give up essential liberty to purchase a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety."

Benjamin Franklin
quoteText: "Those who would give up essential liberty to purchase a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety."

title: To act on the belief that we possess
body:
## "To act on the belief that we possess the knowledge and the power which enable us to shape the processes of society entirely to our liking, knowledge which in fact we do not possess, is likely to make us do much harm."

Fredrich Hayek
quoteText: "To act on the belief that we possess the knowledge and the power which enable us to shape the processes of society entirely to our liking, knowledge which in fact we do not possess, is likely to make us do much harm."

title: To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves
body:
## "To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

title: To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely
body:
## "To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely."

Edmund Burke
quoteText: "To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely."

title: We know they are lying. They know they are lying
body:
## "We know they are lying.
They know they are lying.
They know that we know they are lying.
We know that they know that we know they are lying.
And still they continue to lie." Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
quoteText:
"We know they are lying.
They know they are lying.
They know that we know they are lying.
We know that they know that we know they are lying.
And still they continue to lie."

title: When buying and selling are controlled by legislation
body:
## "When buying and selling are controlled by legislation, the first things to be bought and sold are legislators."

P.J. O'Rourke
quoteText: "When buying and selling are controlled by legislation, the first things to be bought and sold are legislators."

title: When the people fear the government
body:
## "When the people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "When the people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

title: When we lose our individual independence
body:
## "When we lose our individual independence in the corporateness of a mass movement, we find a new freedom — freedom to hate, bully, lie, torture, murder and betray without shame and remorse."

Eric Hoffer
quoteText: "When we lose our individual independence in the corporateness of a mass movement, we find a new freedom — freedom to hate, bully, lie, torture, murder and betray without shame and remorse."

title: You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong
body:
## "You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong."

Abraham Lincoln
quoteText: "You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong."

title: Young people are told at university
body:
## "Young people are told at university that they come from nowhere and belong to nothing: that all pre-existing forms of membership are null and void. They are offered a rite of passage into cultural nothingness, since this is the only way to achieve the egalitarian goal."

Roger Scruton
quoteText: "Young people are told at university that they come from nowhere and belong to nothing: that all pre-existing forms of membership are null and void. They are offered a rite of passage into cultural nothingness, since this is the only way to achieve the egalitarian goal."

title: A free people ought not only to be armed
body:
## "A free people ought not only to be armed, but disciplined."

George Washington
quoteText: "A free people ought not only to be armed, but disciplined."

title: A free press stands as one of the great interpreters
body:
## "A free press stands as one of the great interpreters between the government and the people."

Herbert Hoover
quoteText: "A free press stands as one of the great interpreters between the government and the people."

title: A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy
body:
## "A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy, not a democracy."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy, not a democracy."

title: A government that robs Peter
body:
## "A government that robs Peter to pay Paul can always depend on the support of Paul."

George Bernard Shaw
quoteText: "A government that robs Peter to pay Paul can always depend on the support of Paul."

title: A liberal is someone who feels a great debt
body:
## "A liberal is someone who feels a great debt to his fellow man, which debt he proposes to pay off with your money."

G. Gordon Liddy
quoteText: "A liberal is someone who feels a great debt to his fellow man, which debt he proposes to pay off with your money."

title: A man is no less a slave
body:
## "A man is no less a slave because he is allowed to choose a new master once in a term of years."

Lysander Spooner
quoteText: "A man is no less a slave because he is allowed to choose a new master once in a term of years."

title: A man's admiration for absolute government
body:
## "A man's admiration for absolute government is proportionate to the contempt he feels for those around him."

Alexis de Tocqueville
quoteText: "A man's admiration for absolute government is proportionate to the contempt he feels for those around him."

title: A people who mean to be their own governors
body:
## "A people who mean to be their own governors must arm themselves with the power knowledge gives."

James Madison
quoteText: "A people who mean to be their own governors must arm themselves with the power knowledge gives."

title: A society that puts equality before freedom
body:
## "A society that puts equality before freedom will get neither. A society that puts freedom before equality will get a high degree of both."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "A society that puts equality before freedom will get neither. A society that puts freedom before equality will get a high degree of both."

title: A state that dwarfs its men
body:
## "A state that dwarfs its men, in order that they may be more docile instruments in its hands even for beneficial purposes, will find that with small men no great thing can really be accomplished."

John Stuart Mill
quoteText: "A state that dwarfs its men, in order that they may be more docile instruments in its hands even for beneficial purposes, will find that with small men no great thing can really be accomplished."

title: A wise and frugal government
body:
## "A wise and frugal government shall restrain men from injuring one another, shall leave them otherwise free to regulate their own pursuits of industry and improvement."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "A wise and frugal government shall restrain men from injuring one another, shall leave them otherwise free to regulate their own pursuits of industry and improvement."

title: All great change in America begins at the dinner table
body:
## "All great change in America begins at the dinner table."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "All great change in America begins at the dinner table."

title: All men having power ought to be distrusted
body:
## "All men having power ought to be distrusted to a certain degree."

James Madison
quoteText: "All men having power ought to be distrusted to a certain degree."

title: All socialism involves slavery
body:
## "All socialism involves slavery."

Herbert Spencer
quoteText: "All socialism involves slavery."

title: All that is necessary for evil to triumph
body:
## "All that is necessary for evil to triumph is for good men to do nothing."

Edmund Burke
quoteText: "All that is necessary for evil to triumph is for good men to do nothing."

title: An unlimited power to tax
body:
## "An unlimited power to tax involves, necessarily, a power to destroy."

Daniel Webster
quoteText: "An unlimited power to tax involves, necessarily, a power to destroy."

title: As government expands, liberty contracts
body:
## "As government expands, liberty contracts."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "As government expands, liberty contracts."

title: It constantly amazes me
body:
## "It constantly amazes me that defenders of the free market are expected to offer certainty and perfection, while government has only to make promises and offer good intentions."

Lawrence Reed
quoteText: "It constantly amazes me that defenders of the free market are expected to offer certainty and perfection, while government has only to make promises and offer good intentions."

title: Prior to capitalism
body:
## "Prior to capitalism, the way people amassed great wealth was by looting, plundering and enslaving their fellow man. Capitalism made it possible to become wealthy by serving your fellow man."

Walter E Williams
quoteText: "Prior to capitalism, the way people amassed great wealth was by looting, plundering and enslaving their fellow man. Capitalism made it possible to become wealthy by serving your fellow man."

title: Real Socialism' has never been tried
body:
## "'Real Socialism' has never been tried in the same way that 'Real Capitalism' has never been tried. The difference is 'almost Socialism' resulted in the impoverishment & death of hundreds of millions of people. While 'Almost Capitalism' has lifted billions from absolute poverty."

@Mark Antro
quoteText: "'Real Socialism' has never been tried in the same way that 'Real Capitalism' has never been tried. The difference is 'almost Socialism' resulted in the impoverishment & death of hundreds of millions of people. While 'Almost Capitalism' has lifted billions from absolute poverty."

title: Hayek: The Fatal Conceit
body:
## "The Fatal Conceit: The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design"

Friedrich Hayek
quoteText: "The Fatal Conceit: The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design"

title: Civilization is the process of setting man free
body:
## "Civilization is the process of setting man free from men."

Ayn Rand
quoteText: "Civilization is the process of setting man free from men."

title: Concentrated power is not rendered harmless
body:
## "Concentrated power is not rendered harmless by the good intentions of those who create it."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "Concentrated power is not rendered harmless by the good intentions of those who create it."

title: Concentrated power not harmless
body:
## "Concentrated power is not rendered harmless by the good intentions of those who create it."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "Concentrated power is not rendered harmless by the good intentions of those who create it."

title: Constitution restrains government
body:
## "The Constitution is not an instrument for the government to restrain the people, it is an instrument for the people to restrain the government."

Patrick Henry
quoteText: "The Constitution is not an instrument for the government to restrain the people, it is an instrument for the people to restrain the government."

title: Democracy is two wolves and a lamb
body:
## "Democracy is two wolves and a lamb voting on what to have for lunch. Liberty is a well-armed lamb contesting the vote."

Benjamin Franklin (attributed)
quoteText: "Democracy is two wolves and a lamb voting on what to have for lunch. Liberty is a well-armed lamb contesting the vote."

title: Democracy must be something more
body:
## "Democracy must be something more than two wolves and a sheep voting on what to have for dinner."

James Bovard
quoteText: "Democracy must be something more than two wolves and a sheep voting on what to have for dinner."

title: Equality before freedom
body:
## "A society that puts equality before freedom will get neither. A society that puts freedom before equality will get a high degree of both."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "A society that puts equality before freedom will get neither. A society that puts freedom before equality will get a high degree of both."

title: Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty
body:
## "Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty."

Wendell Phillips
quoteText: "Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty."

title: Every collectivist revolution rides in on a Trojan horse of 'Emergency'
body:
## "Every collectivist revolution rides in on a Trojan horse of 'Emergency'."

Herbert Hoover
quoteText: "Every collectivist revolution rides in on a Trojan horse of 'Emergency'."

title: Every man has a property in his own person
body:
## "Every man has a property in his own person. This nobody has a right to, but himself."

John Locke
quoteText: "Every man has a property in his own person. This nobody has a right to, but himself."

title: Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker
body:
## "Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker of our lives, by that much we move toward making the State our Master."

Dwight D. Eisenhower
quoteText: "Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker of our lives, by that much we move toward making the State our Master."

title: Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state
body:
## "Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state. They forget that the state lives at the expense of everyone."

Frédéric Bastiat
quoteText: "Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state. They forget that the state lives at the expense of everyone."

title: Experience should teach us to be most on our guard
body:
## "Experience should teach us to be most on our guard to protect liberty when the government's purposes are beneficent."

Louis Brandeis
quoteText: "Experience should teach us to be most on our guard to protect liberty when the government's purposes are beneficent."

title: Freedom is never more than one generation
body:
## "Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction."

title: Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor
body:
## "Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor; it must be demanded by the oppressed."

Martin Luther King Jr.
quoteText: "Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor; it must be demanded by the oppressed."

title: Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
body:
## "Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell
quoteText: "Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

title: Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves
body:
## "Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves."

Friedrich Hayek
quoteText: "Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves."

title: Giving money and power to government
body:
## "Giving money and power to government is like giving whiskey and car keys to teenage boys."

P.J. O'Rourke
quoteText: "Giving money and power to government is like giving whiskey and car keys to teenage boys."

title: Giving money and power to government
body:
## "Giving money and power to government is like giving whiskey and car keys to teenage boys."

P.J. O'Rourke
quoteText: "Giving money and power to government is like giving whiskey and car keys to teenage boys."

title: Good intentions will always be pleaded
body:
## "Good intentions will always be pleaded for every assumption of authority. It is hardly too strong to say that the Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions."

Daniel Webster
quoteText: "Good intentions will always be pleaded for every assumption of authority. It is hardly too strong to say that the Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions."

title: Government cannot make man richer
body:
## "Government cannot make man richer, but it can make him poorer."

Ludwig von Mises
quoteText: "Government cannot make man richer, but it can make him poorer."

title: Government has no other end
body:
## "Government has no other end, but the preservation of property."

John Locke
quoteText: "Government has no other end, but the preservation of property."

title: Government is best which governs the least
body:
## "Government is best which governs the least, because its people discipline themselves."

Thomas Jefferson (attributed)
quoteText: "Government is best which governs the least, because its people discipline themselves."

title: Government is not reason; it is not eloquent
body:
## "Government is not reason; it is not eloquent; it is force. Like fire, it is a dangerous servant and a fearful master."

George Washington
quoteText: "Government is not reason; it is not eloquent; it is force. Like fire, it is a dangerous servant and a fearful master."

title: Government is the great fiction
body:
## "Government is the great fiction through which everybody endeavors to live at the expense of everybody else."

Frédéric Bastiat
quoteText: "Government is the great fiction through which everybody endeavors to live at the expense of everybody else."

title: Government’s first duty is to protect the people
body:
## "Government’s first duty is to protect the people, not run their lives."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "Government’s first duty is to protect the people, not run their lives."

title: Government's view of the economy
body:
## "Government's view of the economy could be summed up in a few short phrases: If it moves, tax it. If it keeps moving, regulate it. And if it stops moving, subsidize it."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "Government's view of the economy could be summed up in a few short phrases: If it moves, tax it. If it keeps moving, regulate it. And if it stops moving, subsidize it."

title: He who dares not offend cannot be honest
body:
## "He who dares not offend cannot be honest."

Thomas Paine
quoteText: "He who dares not offend cannot be honest."

title: He who opens a school door, closes a prison
body:
## "He who opens a school door, closes a prison."

Victor Hugo
quoteText: "He who opens a school door, closes a prison."

title: History teaches that war begins
body:
## "History teaches that war begins when governments believe the price of aggression is cheap."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "History teaches that war begins when governments believe the price of aggression is cheap."

title: I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government
body:
## "I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government. It is always oppressive."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government. It is always oppressive."

title: I predict future happiness for Americans
body:
## "I predict future happiness for Americans if they can prevent the government from wasting the labors of the people under the pretense of taking care of them."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "I predict future happiness for Americans if they can prevent the government from wasting the labors of the people under the pretense of taking care of them."

title: I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences
body:
## "I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences attending too much liberty than those attending too small a degree of it."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences attending too much liberty than those attending too small a degree of it."

title: If liberty means anything at all
body:
## "If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell
quoteText: "If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

title: If men were angels
body:
## "If men were angels, no government would be necessary."

James Madison
quoteText: "If men were angels, no government would be necessary."

title: If you have ten thousand regulations
body:
## "If you have ten thousand regulations, you destroy all respect for the law."

Winston Churchill
quoteText: "If you have ten thousand regulations, you destroy all respect for the law."

title: If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert
body:
## "If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert, in five years there'd be a shortage of sand."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert, in five years there'd be a shortage of sand."

title: If you want to help the poor
body:
## "If you want to help the poor, demonstrate to them how they can help themselves."

Benjamin Franklin
quoteText: "If you want to help the poor, demonstrate to them how they can help themselves."

title: In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place
body:
## "In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place."

Mahatma Gandhi
quoteText: "In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place."

title: In politics, stupidity is not a handicap
body:
## "In politics, stupidity is not a handicap."

Napoleon Bonaparte
quoteText: "In politics, stupidity is not a handicap."

title: Inflation is taxation without legislation
body:
## "Inflation is taxation without legislation."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "Inflation is taxation without legislation."

title: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere
body:
## "Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere."

Martin Luther King Jr.
quoteText: "Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere."

title: It is hard to imagine a more stupid
body:
## "It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions than by putting those decisions in the hands of people who pay no price for being wrong."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions than by putting those decisions in the hands of people who pay no price for being wrong."

title: It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law
body:
## "It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law."

Thomas Hobbes
quoteText: "It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law."

title: It is seldom that liberty of any kind is lost all at once
body:
## "It is seldom that liberty of any kind is lost all at once."

David Hume
quoteText: "It is seldom that liberty of any kind is lost all at once."

title: It is the duty of the patriot
body:
## "It is the duty of the patriot to protect his country from its government."

Thomas Paine
quoteText: "It is the duty of the patriot to protect his country from its government."

title: Justice is the end of government
body:
## "Justice is the end of government. It is the end of civil society."

James Madison
quoteText: "Justice is the end of government. It is the end of civil society."

title: Laws that forbid the carrying of arms
body:
## "Laws that forbid the carrying of arms disarm only those who are neither inclined nor determined to commit crimes."

Thomas Jefferson (quoting Cesare Beccaria)
quoteText: "Laws that forbid the carrying of arms disarm only those who are neither inclined nor determined to commit crimes."

title: Let us not seek the Republican answer or the Democratic answer
body:
## "Let us not seek the Republican answer or the Democratic answer, but the right answer."

John F. Kennedy
quoteText: "Let us not seek the Republican answer or the Democratic answer, but the right answer."

title: Liberty is always dangerous
body:
## "Liberty is always dangerous, but it is the safest thing we have."

Harry Emerson Fosdick
quoteText: "Liberty is always dangerous, but it is the safest thing we have."

title: Liberty is not a means to a higher political end
body:
## "Liberty is not a means to a higher political end. It is itself the highest political end."

Lord Acton
quoteText: "Liberty is not a means to a higher political end. It is itself the highest political end."

title: Liberty is not collective, it is personal
body:
## "Liberty is not collective, it is personal. All liberty is individual liberty."

Calvin Coolidge
quoteText: "Liberty is not collective, it is personal. All liberty is individual liberty."

title: Liberty is not the power of doing what we like
body:
## "Liberty is not the power of doing what we like, but the right of being able to do what we ought."

Lord Acton
quoteText: "Liberty is not the power of doing what we like, but the right of being able to do what we ought."

title: Liberty means responsibility
body:
## "Liberty means responsibility. That is why most men dread it."

George Bernard Shaw
quoteText: "Liberty means responsibility. That is why most men dread it."

title: Liberty means responsibility
body:
## "Liberty means responsibility. That is why most men dread it."

George Bernard Shaw
quoteText: "Liberty means responsibility. That is why most men dread it."

title: Liberty must at all hazards be supported
body:
## "Liberty must at all hazards be supported. We have a right to it, derived from our Maker."

John Adams
quoteText: "Liberty must at all hazards be supported. We have a right to it, derived from our Maker."

title: Man is not free unless government is limited
body:
## "Man is not free unless government is limited."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "Man is not free unless government is limited."

title: Man is not made for the state
body:
## "Man is not made for the state. The state is made for man."

Albert Einstein
quoteText: "Man is not made for the state. The state is made for man."

title: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful citizens
body:
## "Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world. Indeed, it is the only thing that ever has."

Margaret Mead
quoteText: "Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world. Indeed, it is the only thing that ever has."

title: Never let a crisis go to waste
body:
## "Never let a crisis go to waste. They are opportunities to do big things."

Rahm Emanuel
quoteText: "Never let a crisis go to waste. They are opportunities to do big things."

title: Nine most terrifying words
body:
## "The nine most terrifying words in the English language are: I'm from the government and I'm here to help."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "The nine most terrifying words in the English language are: I'm from the government and I'm here to help."

title: No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms
body:
## "No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms."

title: No government can exist without taxation
body:
## "No government can exist without taxation. This money must necessarily be drawn from the pockets of the people."

John Marshall
quoteText: "No government can exist without taxation. This money must necessarily be drawn from the pockets of the people."

title: No government ever voluntarily reduces itself
body:
## "No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size."

title: No government ever voluntarily reduces itself
body:
## "No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size. Government programs, once launched, never disappear."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size. Government programs, once launched, never disappear."

title: No man's life, liberty or property is safe
body:
## "No man's life, liberty or property is safe while the legislature is in session."

Gideon J. Tucker
quoteText: "No man's life, liberty or property is safe while the legislature is in session."

title: No man's life, liberty, or property
body:
## "No man's life, liberty, or property are safe while the legislature is in session."

Mark Twain
quoteText: "No man's life, liberty, or property are safe while the legislature is in session."

title: None are more hopelessly enslaved
body:
## "None are more hopelessly enslaved than those who falsely believe they are free."

Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
quoteText: "None are more hopelessly enslaved than those who falsely believe they are free."

title: None can love freedom heartily
body:
## "None can love freedom heartily but good men; the rest love not freedom but license."

John Milton
quoteText: "None can love freedom heartily but good men; the rest love not freedom but license."

title: Nothing is more destructive of individual character
body:
## "Nothing is more destructive of individual character than being raised in dependence on the government."

Alexis de Tocqueville
quoteText: "Nothing is more destructive of individual character than being raised in dependence on the government."

title: Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure
body:
## "Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure."

title: Nothing is so permanent
body:
## "Nothing is so permanent as a temporary government program."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "Nothing is so permanent as a temporary government program."

title: Of all the enemies to public liberty
body:
## "Of all the enemies to public liberty, war is, perhaps, the most to be dreaded."

James Madison
quoteText: "Of all the enemies to public liberty, war is, perhaps, the most to be dreaded."

title: Of all tyrannies
body:
## "Of all tyrannies, a tyranny sincerely exercised for the good of its victims may be the most oppressive."

C.S. Lewis
quoteText: "Of all tyrannies, a tyranny sincerely exercised for the good of its victims may be the most oppressive."

title: Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom
body:
## "Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom. As nations become corrupt and vicious, they have more need of masters."

Benjamin Franklin
quoteText: "Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom. As nations become corrupt and vicious, they have more need of masters."

title: Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people
body:
## "Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people. It is wholly inadequate to the government of any other."

John Adams
quoteText: "Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people. It is wholly inadequate to the government of any other."

title: People who are willing to trade freedom for security
body:
## "People who are willing to trade freedom for security will end up with neither."

Benjamin Franklin
quoteText: "People who are willing to trade freedom for security will end up with neither."

title: Political correctness is tyranny
body:
## "Political correctness is tyranny with manners."

Charlton Heston
quoteText: "Political correctness is tyranny with manners."

title: Power tends to corrupt
body:
## "Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely."

Lord Acton
quoteText: "Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely."

title: Power to tax is power to destroy
body:
## "The power to tax involves the power to destroy."

John Marshall
quoteText: "The power to tax involves the power to destroy."

title: Socialism is a philosophy of failure
body:
## "Socialism is a philosophy of failure, the creed of ignorance, and the gospel of envy."

Winston Churchill
quoteText: "Socialism is a philosophy of failure, the creed of ignorance, and the gospel of envy."

title: That government is best which governs least
body:
## "That government is best which governs least."

Henry David Thoreau
quoteText: "That government is best which governs least."

title: The advancement and diffusion of knowledge
body:
## "The advancement and diffusion of knowledge is the only guardian of true liberty."

James Madison
quoteText: "The advancement and diffusion of knowledge is the only guardian of true liberty."

title: The bigger the government, the more it can give you
body:
## "The bigger the government, the more it can give you everything you want — and take away everything you have."

Anonymous Conservative Axiom
quoteText: "The bigger the government, the more it can give you everything you want — and take away everything you have."

title: The bigger the government, the smaller the citizen
body:
## "The bigger the government, the smaller the citizen."

Dennis Prager
quoteText: "The bigger the government, the smaller the citizen."

title: The Constitution is not a living organism
body:
## "The Constitution is not a living organism. It's a legal document, and it says what it says and doesn't say what it doesn't say."

Antonin Scalia
quoteText: "The Constitution is not a living organism. It's a legal document, and it says what it says and doesn't say what it doesn't say."

title: The Constitution is not an instrument for the government
body:
## "The Constitution is not an instrument for the government to restrain the people, it is an instrument for the people to restrain the government."

Patrick Henry
quoteText: "The Constitution is not an instrument for the government to restrain the people, it is an instrument for the people to restrain the government."

title: The Constitution is not neutral
body:
## "The Constitution is not neutral. It was designed to take the government off the backs of people."

William O. Douglas
quoteText: "The Constitution is not neutral. It was designed to take the government off the backs of people."

title: The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness
body:
## "The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness. You have to catch it yourself."

Benjamin Franklin
quoteText: "The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness. You have to catch it yourself."

title: The Constitution was made to guard the people
body:
## "The Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions."

Daniel Webster
quoteText: "The Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions."

title: The curious task of economics
body:
## "The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design."

Friedrich Hayek
quoteText: "The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design."

title: The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority
body:
## "The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority but tyranny by the majority."

James Madison
quoteText: "The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority but tyranny by the majority."

title: The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern
body:
## "The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern. Every class is unfit to govern."

Lord Acton
quoteText: "The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern. Every class is unfit to govern."

title: The difference between a welfare state and a totalitarian state
body:
## "The difference between a welfare state and a totalitarian state is a matter of time."

Ayn Rand
quoteText: "The difference between a welfare state and a totalitarian state is a matter of time."

title: The essence of freedom
body:
## "The essence of freedom is the proper limitation of government."

Founding Fathers
quoteText: "The essence of freedom is the proper limitation of government."

title: The evils of tyranny are rarely seen
body:
## "The evils of tyranny are rarely seen but by him who resists it."

John Hay
quoteText: "The evils of tyranny are rarely seen but by him who resists it."

title: The first duty of society is justice
body:
## "The first duty of society is justice."

Alexander Hamilton
quoteText: "The first duty of society is justice."

title: The freedom to fail is vital
body:
## "The freedom to fail is vital if we are to have the freedom to succeed."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "The freedom to fail is vital if we are to have the freedom to succeed."

title: The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted
body:
## "The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted; it belongs to the brave."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted; it belongs to the brave."

title: The goal of socialism is communism
body:
## "The goal of socialism is communism."

Vladimir Lenin
quoteText: "The goal of socialism is communism."

title: The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem
body:
## "The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem."

title: The government was set to protect man
body:
## "The government was set to protect man from criminals and the Constitution was written to protect man from the government."

Ayn Rand
quoteText: "The government was set to protect man from criminals and the Constitution was written to protect man from the government."

title: The great danger in republics
body:
## "The great danger in republics is that the majority may not sufficiently respect the rights of the minority."

James Madison
quoteText: "The great danger in republics is that the majority may not sufficiently respect the rights of the minority."

title: The great danger to liberty
body:
## "The great danger to liberty is that the state will come to regard all liberty as a threat to itself."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The great danger to liberty is that the state will come to regard all liberty as a threat to itself."

title: The history of liberty is a history of resistance
body:
## "The history of liberty is a history of resistance."

Woodrow Wilson
quoteText: "The history of liberty is a history of resistance."

title: The inherent vice of capitalism
body:
## "The inherent vice of capitalism is the unequal sharing of blessings; the inherent virtue of socialism is the equal sharing of miseries."

Winston Churchill
quoteText: "The inherent vice of capitalism is the unequal sharing of blessings; the inherent virtue of socialism is the equal sharing of miseries."

title: The issue is always the same
body:
## "The issue is always the same: the individual versus the collective."

Ayn Rand
quoteText: "The issue is always the same: the individual versus the collective."

title: The legitimate object of government
body:
## "The legitimate object of government is to do for a community of people whatever they need to have done but cannot do at all in their separate and individual capacities."

Abraham Lincoln
quoteText: "The legitimate object of government is to do for a community of people whatever they need to have done but cannot do at all in their separate and individual capacities."

title: The legitimate powers of government extend
body:
## "The legitimate powers of government extend to such acts only as are injurious to others."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "The legitimate powers of government extend to such acts only as are injurious to others."

title: The liberties of a people never were
body:
## "The liberties of a people never were, nor ever will be, secure when the transactions of their rulers may be concealed from them."

Patrick Henry
quoteText: "The liberties of a people never were, nor ever will be, secure when the transactions of their rulers may be concealed from them."

title: The liberty of the individual must be thus far limited
body:
## "The liberty of the individual must be thus far limited; he must not make himself a nuisance to other people."

John Stuart Mill
quoteText: "The liberty of the individual must be thus far limited; he must not make himself a nuisance to other people."

title: The man who is not allowed to own property
body:
## "The man who is not allowed to own property is a man reduced to dependence."

John Locke
quoteText: "The man who is not allowed to own property is a man reduced to dependence."

title: The man who reads nothing
body:
## "The man who reads nothing at all is better educated than the man who reads nothing but newspapers."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "The man who reads nothing at all is better educated than the man who reads nothing but newspapers."

title: The man who trades freedom for security
body:
## "The man who trades freedom for security does not deserve nor will he ever receive either."

Benjamin Franklin
quoteText: "The man who trades freedom for security does not deserve nor will he ever receive either."

title: The more corrupt the state
body:
## "The more corrupt the state, the more numerous the laws."

Tacitus
quoteText: "The more corrupt the state, the more numerous the laws."

title: The more laws, the less justice
body:
## "The more laws, the less justice."

Marcus Tullius Cicero
quoteText: "The more laws, the less justice."

title: The more the state plans
body:
## "The more the state plans, the more difficult planning becomes for the individual."

Friedrich Hayek
quoteText: "The more the state plans, the more difficult planning becomes for the individual."

title: The most dangerous man to any government
body:
## "The most dangerous man to any government is the man who is able to think things out for himself."

H.L. Mencken
quoteText: "The most dangerous man to any government is the man who is able to think things out for himself."

title: The natural liberty of man
body:
## "The natural liberty of man is to be free from any superior power on Earth."

Samuel Adams
quoteText: "The natural liberty of man is to be free from any superior power on Earth."

title: The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield
body:
## "The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield and government to gain ground."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield and government to gain ground."

title: The nine most terrifying words
body:
## "The nine most terrifying words in the English language are: I'm from the government and I'm here to help."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "The nine most terrifying words in the English language are: I'm from the government and I'm here to help."

title: The only freedom which deserves the name
body:
## "The only freedom which deserves the name is that of pursuing our own good in our own way."

John Stuart Mill
quoteText: "The only freedom which deserves the name is that of pursuing our own good in our own way."

title: The only thing that saves us from bureaucracy
body:
## "The only thing that saves us from the bureaucracy is inefficiency. An efficient bureaucracy is the greatest threat to liberty."

Eugene McCarthy
quoteText: "The only thing that saves us from the bureaucracy is inefficiency. An efficient bureaucracy is the greatest threat to liberty."

title: The people are the only sure reliance
body:
## "The people are the only sure reliance for the preservation of our liberty."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "The people are the only sure reliance for the preservation of our liberty."

title: The philosophy of the schoolroom in one generation
body:
## "The philosophy of the schoolroom in one generation will be the philosophy of government in the next."

Abraham Lincoln
quoteText: "The philosophy of the schoolroom in one generation will be the philosophy of government in the next."

title: The power to tax involves the power to destroy
body:
## "The power to tax involves the power to destroy."

John Marshall
quoteText: "The power to tax involves the power to destroy."

title: The power under the Constitution will always be in the people
body:
## "The power under the Constitution will always be in the people."

George Washington
quoteText: "The power under the Constitution will always be in the people."

title: The price of freedom is eternal vigilance
body:
## "The price of freedom is eternal vigilance."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "The price of freedom is eternal vigilance."

title: The problem is not that people are taxed too little
body:
## "The problem is not that people are taxed too little, the problem is that government spends too much."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "The problem is not that people are taxed too little, the problem is that government spends too much."

title: The problem is not to find better people
body:
## "The problem is not to find better people to govern, but to allow a system that lets the governed govern themselves."

Ludwig von Mises
quoteText: "The problem is not to find better people to govern, but to allow a system that lets the governed govern themselves."

title: The problem isn't that Johnny can't read
body:
## "The problem isn't that Johnny can't read. The problem isn't even that Johnny can't think. The problem is that Johnny doesn't know what thinking is."

Richard Mitchell
quoteText: "The problem isn't that Johnny can't read. The problem isn't even that Johnny can't think. The problem is that Johnny doesn't know what thinking is."

title: The problem with political jokes
body:
## "The problem with political jokes is they get elected."

Anonymous

title: The problem with socialism
body:
## "The problem with socialism is that you eventually run out of other people's money."

Margaret Thatcher
quoteText: "The problem with socialism is that you eventually run out of other people's money."

title: The progress of society depends not on legislation
body:
## "The progress of society depends not on legislation, but on the moral sense of individuals."

William E. Gladstone
quoteText: "The progress of society depends not on legislation, but on the moral sense of individuals."

title: The purpose of a constitution
body:
## "The purpose of a constitution is to restrain the government, not the people."

Patrick Henry
quoteText: "The purpose of a constitution is to restrain the government, not the people."

title: The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries
body:
## "The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries but between authoritarians and libertarians."

George Orwell
quoteText: "The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries but between authoritarians and libertarians."

title: The reason every Marxist government
body:
## "The reason every Marxist government in the history of the world turned massively repressive is not because they all had the misfortune of being hijacked by murderous thugs. It's that the ideology itself prioritizes class justice over individual rights and makes no allowance for legitimate disagreement."

Jonathan Chait
quoteText: "The reason every Marxist government in the history of the world turned massively repressive is not because they all had the misfortune of being hijacked by murderous thugs. It's that the ideology itself prioritizes class justice over individual rights and makes no allowance for legitimate disagreement."

title: The right to be let alone

## "The right to be let alone is the most comprehensive of rights, and the right most valued by a free people."

Louis Brandeis
quoteText: "The right to be let alone is the most comprehensive of rights, and the right most valued by a free people."

title: The rights of individuals should be the primary object
body:
## "The rights of individuals should be the primary object of all governments."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "The rights of individuals should be the primary object of all governments."

title: The rights of man come not from the generosity of the state
body:
## "The rights of man come not from the generosity of the state but from the hand of God."

John F. Kennedy
quoteText: "The rights of man come not from the generosity of the state but from the hand of God."

title: The rule of law is the most civilized concept
body:
## "The rule of law is the most civilized concept humanity has yet devised."

Margaret Thatcher
quoteText: "The rule of law is the most civilized concept humanity has yet devised."

title: The smallest minority on earth is the individual
body:
## "The smallest minority on earth is the individual. Those who deny individual rights cannot claim to be defenders of minorities."

Ayn Rand
quoteText: "The smallest minority on earth is the individual. Those who deny individual rights cannot claim to be defenders of minorities."

title: The state is a servant of the people
body:
## "The state is a servant of the people. It must never become an absolute master."

Vaclav Havel
quoteText: "The state is a servant of the people. It must never become an absolute master."

title: The state is not equipped to run businesses
body:
## "The state is not equipped to run businesses. It is equipped to run the law."

Margaret Thatcher
quoteText: "The state is not equipped to run businesses. It is equipped to run the law."

title: The state is that great fiction
body:
## "The state is that great fiction by which everyone tries to live at the expense of everyone else."

Frédéric Bastiat
quoteText: "The state is that great fiction by which everyone tries to live at the expense of everyone else."

title: The tree of liberty must be refreshed
body:
## "The tree of liberty must be refreshed from time to time with the blood of patriots and tyrants."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "The tree of liberty must be refreshed from time to time with the blood of patriots and tyrants."

title: The trouble with socialism
body:
## "The trouble with socialism is that it always ends up running out of other people’s money."

Margaret Thatcher
quoteText: "The trouble with socialism is that it always ends up running out of other people’s money."

title: The true danger is when liberty is nibbled away
body:
## "The true danger is when liberty is nibbled away, for expedients, and by parts."

Edmund Burke
quoteText: "The true danger is when liberty is nibbled away, for expedients, and by parts."

title: The ultimate authority resides in the people alone
body:
## "The ultimate authority resides in the people alone."

James Madison
quoteText: "The ultimate authority resides in the people alone."

title: The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly
body:
## "The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly is to fill the world with fools."

Herbert Spencer
quoteText: "The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly is to fill the world with fools."

title: The urge to save humanity
body:
## "The urge to save humanity is almost always a false front for the urge to rule."

H.L. Mencken
quoteText: "The urge to save humanity is almost always a false front for the urge to rule."

title: The welfare of humanity is always the alibi of tyrants
body:
## "The welfare of humanity is always the alibi of tyrants."

Albert Camus
quoteText: "The welfare of humanity is always the alibi of tyrants."

title: The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses
body:
## "The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses. It is about the egos of the elites."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses. It is about the egos of the elites."

title: The welfare state is the oldest con game
body:
## "The welfare state is the oldest con game in the world. First you take people’s money away quietly and then you give some of it back to them flamboyantly."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "The welfare state is the oldest con game in the world. First you take people’s money away quietly and then you give some of it back to them flamboyantly."

title: The will of the people is not always the law
body:
## "The will of the people is not always the law. The law should protect the rights of the individual, even against the majority."

Barry Goldwater
quoteText: "The will of the people is not always the law. The law should protect the rights of the individual, even against the majority."

title: The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure
body:
## "The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure were inflicted by bad governments."

Ludwig von Mises
quoteText: "The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure were inflicted by bad governments."

title: There are no necessary evils in government
body:
## "There are no necessary evils in government. Its evils exist only in its abuses."

Andrew Jackson
quoteText: "There are no necessary evils in government. Its evils exist only in its abuses."

title: There are no solutions, only trade-offs
body:
## "There are no solutions, only trade-offs."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "There are no solutions, only trade-offs."

title: There is all the difference in the world
body:
## "There is all the difference in the world between treating people equally and attempting to make them equal."

Friedrich Hayek
quoteText: "There is all the difference in the world between treating people equally and attempting to make them equal."

title: There is no liberty without independence of mind
body:
## "There is no liberty without independence of mind."

Alexis de Tocqueville
quoteText: "There is no liberty without independence of mind."

title: There is no such thing as a free lunch
body:
## "There is no such thing as a free lunch."

Milton Friedman
quoteText: "There is no such thing as a free lunch."

title: There is no worse tyranny
body:
## "There is no worse tyranny than to force a man to pay for what he does not want merely because you think it would be good for him."

Robert A. Heinlein
quoteText: "There is no worse tyranny than to force a man to pay for what he does not want merely because you think it would be good for him."

title: Those who cry out that the government should 'do something'
body:
## "Those who cry out that the government should 'do something' never even ask what the government is qualified to do."

Thomas Sowell
quoteText: "Those who cry out that the government should 'do something' never even ask what the government is qualified to do."

title: Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom
body:
## "Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom must, like men, undergo the fatigue of supporting it."

Thomas Paine
quoteText: "Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom must, like men, undergo the fatigue of supporting it."

title: To be controlled in our economic pursuits
body:
## "To be controlled in our economic pursuits means to be controlled in everything."

Friedrich Hayek
quoteText: "To be controlled in our economic pursuits means to be controlled in everything."

title: To compel a man to furnish contributions
body:
## "To compel a man to furnish contributions of money for the propagation of opinions which he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "To compel a man to furnish contributions of money for the propagation of opinions which he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

title: To compel a man to subsidize
body:
## "To compel a man to subsidize the teaching of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "To compel a man to subsidize the teaching of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

title: To educate a man in mind and not in morals
body:
## "To educate a man in mind and not in morals is to educate a menace to society."

Theodore Roosevelt
quoteText: "To educate a man in mind and not in morals is to educate a menace to society."

title: To live under the American Constitution
body:
## "To live under the American Constitution is the greatest political privilege that was ever accorded to the human race."

Calvin Coolidge
quoteText: "To live under the American Constitution is the greatest political privilege that was ever accorded to the human race."

title: To sin by silence when they should protest
body:
## "To sin by silence when they should protest makes cowards of men."

Ella Wheeler Wilcox
quoteText: "To sin by silence when they should protest makes cowards of men."

title: Truth is treason in the empire of lies
body:
## "Truth is treason in the empire of lies."

Ron Paul
quoteText: "Truth is treason in the empire of lies."

title: Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government
body:
## "Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government but illegal for the citizenry."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government but illegal for the citizenry."

title: We are a nation that has a government
body:
## "We are a nation that has a government - not the other way around."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "We are a nation that has a government - not the other way around."

title: We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty
body:
## "We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty in a featherbed."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty in a featherbed."

title: What is not just is not law
body:
## "What is not just is not law."

William Lloyd Garrison
quoteText: "What is not just is not law."

title: What is prudence in the conduct of every private family
body:
## "What is prudence in the conduct of every private family can scarce be folly in that of a great kingdom."

Adam Smith
quoteText: "What is prudence in the conduct of every private family can scarce be folly in that of a great kingdom."

title: When all men are paid for existing
body:
## "When all men are paid for existing and no man must pay for his sins, then the Party is over."

G.K. Chesterton
quoteText: "When all men are paid for existing and no man must pay for his sins, then the Party is over."

title: When buying and selling are controlled by legislation
body:
## "When buying and selling are controlled by legislation, the first things to be bought and sold are legislators."

P.J. O'Rourke
quoteText: "When buying and selling are controlled by legislation, the first things to be bought and sold are legislators."

title: When injustice becomes law
body:
## "When injustice becomes law, resistance becomes duty."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "When injustice becomes law, resistance becomes duty."

title: When people fear the government, there is tyranny
body:
## "When people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "When people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

title: When the people fear the government
body:
## "When the people fear the government, there is tyranny. When the government fears the people, there is liberty."

Thomas Jefferson
quoteText: "When the people fear the government, there is tyranny. When the government fears the people, there is liberty."

title: When the people find they can vote themselves money
body:
## "When the people find they can vote themselves money, that will herald the end of the republic."

Benjamin Franklin (attributed)
quoteText: "When the people find they can vote themselves money, that will herald the end of the republic."

title: Without liberty, law loses its nature
body:
## "Without liberty, law loses its nature and justice loses its meaning."

Alexander Hamilton
quoteText: "Without liberty, law loses its nature and justice loses its meaning."

title: You cannot multiply wealth
body:
## "You cannot multiply wealth by dividing it."

Adrian Rogers
quoteText: "You cannot multiply wealth by dividing it."

title: You can’t be for big government
body:
## "You can’t be for big government, big taxes, and big bureaucracy and still be for the little guy."

Ronald Reagan
quoteText: "You can’t be for big government, big taxes, and big bureaucracy and still be for the little guy."

title: You can’t legislate morality
body:
## "You can’t legislate morality. You can only regulate behavior."

Clarence Thomas
quoteText: "You can’t legislate morality. You can only regulate behavior."
